items.title: [正面] I've officially cleared the honeymoon phase where ...
items.summary:
I've officially cleared the honeymoon phase where the freebies end, and I am now out of resources to continue growing my account nicely. Here is what I can tell you about this game from a purely subjective standpoint:

I will start off with my light criticisims:

When I picked up this game, I was sold on it being a "Slay The Spire" + "Lovecraftian horror" + "All characters are good" game. What I found instead was "Slay the relics" + "Memory Goo Horror" + "Banner characters are good". For that last statement, Doll is my knockout blow to those who will argue my point. Her ultimate is super depressing. Can heal more with ANY Caro on the team (bar skill), and more consistently.

One character who's design I really liked was Faros. A lighthouse Lovecraftian horror character that uses tentacles to attack and doom-stack insane amounts of poison? Yes please! And a draw 3? Yes again! However, what makes her weak is the draw 3 "from draw pile" Ultimate. Which can cripple its effectiveness. Also the cards will remain full cost, so you need to save your Mouse Posse for 3 mana to maybe play one or two of the 3 you pull from the deck (Unless for some reason you wanna play 3 strike/defend cards).  Why not just use it on turn start? That sounds like a good idea, till you remember my first point: "ONLY from draw pile.". If you had a card you wanted to play, but it was discarded to the discard pile, You have to wait till it is EVENTUALLY recycled back into the draw pile. Better hope you sold as many cards as you could and did not pick up any symptom cards! All in all, her Ultimate needs a rework. That shield she gets as well is not even worth mentioning when grunts can get over it easily.

Now that I got that out of my system, the good:

The writing and art are amazing. Surreal and dark yet compelling all at the same time. The music and sound effects really are well made too. You can FEEL when attacks hit right, and the musical queues fit in nicely to the mood. The UI is wonky at times (probably that machine-translation still needing to be worked out.) but its OK. Not impossible to read most of the time (You will be relying on the text-log button a lot to read the full text sometimes.)

And the bread and butter of course: The deck building. Each character has 5 cards and an ultimate. But in reality, most might as well have 3 cards and an ultimate, as I don't consider basic strike and defense cards as "cards". I usually dump those in the shop for some cash first chance I get (Unless the build is reliant around those cards.). The complexity comes in with the other 3 card and the ultimate. And those are made MORE complex by the 2 "Wheel of Destiny" cards you equip to each of your characters. And then you can equip 6 "Covenant" shards as well!. The idea was to make it so that any character can be viable with the right build. And for the most part: it works! Especially if you get the character paired with their specific "Wheel of Destiny.". Again, 2 of the 3 initial characters you get right away are just bad, and the other 1 completely unusable (Doll). But so long as you use any of the other characters (Yes, even bottom tier Faros), you can make builds that synergize with them to erase weaknesses or build on strengths.

The rougelike system also allows for massive experimentation and replayability. Its arguably one of its biggest strengths to keep things fresh. You can get bored of playing with the same deck, but what if in your next run you get an item that makes your defense cards give you counter stacks? You now suddenly make your poison DPS into a hybrid counter poison deck. Lots of fun to be had on that front.

TLDR: Lovecraftian Horror Slay The Spire Anime Gacha Game with rougelike systems. PVP is purely optional, and you can get PVP rewards in daily dungons if you don't like PVP. 8/10 (Buff the starter characters and Faros Ultimate)

items.title: [正面] 剧情可圈可点，摸鱼打牌感觉不错，孩子很爱玩
